A practical disbelief in God's rule was at the root of all Israel’s wickedness; as if God could not see it or did not care about it. Their sins are everywhere around them. Their hearts were filled with evil desires, like a heated oven. In the midst of their troubles as a nation, the people never thought of seeking help from God. The actual wickedness of men's lives is very small compared to what is in their hearts. But when lust is secretly cherished, it will break out into outward sin. Those who tempt others to drunkenness cannot be their real friends and often plan their ruin. Thus, men carry out God’s vengeance on each other. Those are not only filled with sin but made hard by sin, who continue to live without prayer, even when in trouble and distress.

Israel was like a cake that wasn't flipped, half burnt and half dough, none of it fit for use; a mixture of idolatry and worship of the Lord. There were signs of approaching ruin, as grey hairs are of old age, but they did not notice them. The pride that leads to breaking God’s law leads to self-praise. The mercy and grace of God are the only refuge to which stubborn sinners never think of fleeing. Though they may express their fears in the form of prayers, they seldom cry out to God with their hearts. Even their prayers for earthly blessings only seek fuel for their desires. Their turning from one group, belief, practice, or vice to another still leaves them far short of Christ and holiness. Such are we by nature. And such we will be if left to ourselves. Create in us a clean heart, O God, and renew a right spirit within us.
